title: Global Feature Analysis and Comparative Evaluation of Freestyle In-Air-Handwriting Passcode for User Authentication

method.input_representation: six-degree-of-freedom rigid body motion of a point in 3D space (18 axes)
method.training_strategy: Per-account SVM training using registration signals as positive samples and random signals from other accounts as negative samples.

evaluation.context: Achieved by T-Fusion(E) using Camera device (without collision)
Achieved by T-Fusion(E) using Camera device (without collision) at FAR = 10^-3
Achieved by T-Fusion(E) using Glove device (without collision) at FRR = 0

evaluation_protocol: Five repetitions for registration; five repetitions for login requests. Evaluated 'without collision' (random guessing) and 'with collision' (impostors knowing passcode content).

reported_limitations: Camera tracking degrades during fast hand movement or certain poses
Template quality is limited by few registration samples
Long-term behavior variation leads to increased FRR without updates

usability.usability_issues: Requirement to wear a glove; camera field-of-view restrictions.

key_contribution: Unified framework for IMU and camera-based in-air handwriting
Detailed analysis of temporal and statistical features for behavioral biometrics
main_strength: Comparative analysis of multiple fusion strategies (S-Fusion vs F-Fusion)
main_weakness: Data collected entirely in a lab setting
High dependence on template updates to maintain performance
insights.notes_for_review: The system behaves more like a password system with a biometric layer than a pure biometric system, as the content secret is primary for security.
